name: catenator
short_description: A Python tool for concatenating code files from a directory into a single output string.
long_description: Catenator is a command-line utility and Python library that concatenates source code files from a specified directory into a single output file or stream. It supports multiple programming languages and provides advanced features including directory tree generation, README file inclusion, gitignore-style .catignore pattern filtering, YAML-based build configurations, token counting, and file watching. The tool automatically ranks files by importance and can summarize less critical files when output exceeds token limits, making it ideal for preparing codebases for analysis by language models, documentation generation, or code review.

modules.description: Package initialization exporting the Catenator class and defining project metadata (version, author, description).
Core module implementing the Catenator class with file concatenation, tree generation, extension filtering, .catignore and YAML build configuration parsing, file watching, and token counting.
File importance ranking and summarization module that uses heuristics to rank files and generates summaries via docstring extraction or AI when output exceeds token limits.
Default gitignore-style patterns to exclude common non-code files from concatenation (node_modules, __pycache__, build, etc).
Unit tests covering concatenation, tree generation, extension filtering, .catignore parsing, build configurations, and token counting functionality.
Package setup configuration defining dependencies, console entry points, and distribution metadata.

features: Multiple language support (py, js, java, c, cpp, go, rust, etc)
File watching with automatic output updates
Token limit enforcement with automatic summarization
author: Philip Orange
